metadata.title:
Declares Proposed Provision Is Unfair Discrimination
Against Local Enterprise as Compared to Exclusive
Franchises Granted Other Companies and That Project
Cannot Be Undertaken if Such Condition Is Imposed.

pages.raw_text:

losing proposition for us. Our
work and money would be prac-
tically thrown away.


"The argument in favor Of the
joint user clause in this instance
is based on the erroneous idea that
but one subway can be built in Spo-
kane. In the event that some other


line wished a subway under the
business district there is still left


Main, Riverside and Sprague avs.
These with Front av. were avail
able for subway purposes and we
selected Front.


"If the other companies need
subways are they not just as able


to build them as we are? II they


build subways it is because of the


benefits to be derived by the build
err. Why should not the beneficiary


pay the cost of construction? Is it


fair to compel our company to bear
all this expense for other lines to


profit by simply because we are the


pioneers in this enterprise?


"The subway system for city


railroads Is universally accepted as


the real solution Of the traffic prob


lem on crowded streets. 1t is safer


and more convenient for the pub


lie as we'll as the company. This
| plan has been followed in New


York, Seattle and numerous other


cities l might name. Spokane has


reached the period in her growth


when a subway is necessary at least
for our business. We propose TO


build it and ask nothing but the
right to do so. The council can
burden us with conditions that will


make the enterprise planned impos-
sible If it wishes, but what is there


to be gained by that? 1t merely


means the smothering of an enter


prise which We may modestly claim


means as much to Spokane as any
thing ever undertaken here"


Corporation Counsel James Ger
, agility said today that the advocates
, of the joint or common user clause
| in the subway franchise were not


actuated by any feeling of hostility


to the Graves interests but is due
to the fact that the Graves people
have secured both sides of the river
and that they could control abso-
, lutely the outlet down the river for
all time If granted the franchise
, asked.


He also said that Frank P.
Graves had repeatedly stated that


the franchises now owned by the
Washington Water Power Co.
should have had joint user clause.
